servinfosub.title: Irregular and/or Premature Wear
servinfosub.ptxt: Irregular and/or premature wear occurs due to incorrect inflation pressures, lack of tire rotation, rough driving, improper alignment. If one of the following conditions is noted, rotation is necessary.

item.ptxt: Front tires wear differently from rear tires.
Uneven wear between both sides of tread on the same tire.
Abnormal wear such as cupping and flat spotting is noted.

servinfosub.ptxt: Wheel alignment should be checked if one of the following conditions is noted.

item.ptxt: Uneven wear between both sides of tread on the same front tire.
Front tire treads show scuffs with “feather” edges on one side of tread ribs or blocks.

entry.ptxt: Hard cornering, under inflation or non-rotated tires
Incorrect wheel alignment, use of tires of different types or wheel heavy acceleration

servinfosub.title: Wear Indicator
servinfosub.ptxt: Originally installed tires have built-in tread wear indicators (1).

These indicators appear as 7 mm (0.27 in) wide bands when the tire tread depth becomes 1.6 mm (0.063 in). If a tire has indicators (1) appearing across 3 or more tread grooves at 6 locations, replacement of the tire is recommended.
